title: Joe Mazzulla’s ‘tough love’ reminds Robert Parish of a particular Hall-of-Fame coach
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Robert Parish compares Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla's style to former coach Bill Fitch's 'tough love' approach.
Parish highlights how Mazzulla's martial arts background influences his coaching intensity and confidence.
Mazzulla praised for managing Celtics' lineup effectively, fostering team confidence during Jayson Tatum's absence.

title: Lukas Reichel has a tall task sparking dormant part of Bruins’ lineup
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Lukas Reichel joins the Boston Bruins' third line for Thursday's game against Winnipeg to boost the team's offense.
Reichel, a 2020 first-round pick, has played 188 NHL games but is seeking consistency with the Bruins.
Acquired in a trade with Vancouver for a 2026 sixth-round pick, Reichel impressed with 5 points in 3 AHL games.

title: A Massachusetts police officer is running the Boston Marathon for veterans
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Laicey Ieronimo, a 25-year-old Massachusetts police officer, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for veterans.
Ieronimo is supporting Semper Fi & America's Fund, honoring veterans including her friends and family.
She has previously run the marathon for other charities and has raised over $40,000 through various races.

title: Cambridge Day founder Marc Levy is back with a new Cambridge and Somerville news hub
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Marc Levy launched the Cambridge Somerville Independent to provide local news coverage for Cambridge and Somerville.
The publication debuted online February 12 and covers local government, arts, and community events.
Cambridge Somerville Independent uses a hybrid model, combining commercial ads with donations through the Somerville Media Fund.

title: For this Boston Marathon runner, the starting line is everything
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Marine veteran Gregory Baxter runs Boston Marathon to honor resilience and support fellow service members.
Baxter, 43, from Laguna Niguel, Calif., sees the starting line as a representation of the journey and its challenges.
He aims to raise awareness and funds for Semper Fi & America's Fund, benefiting military personnel and families.

title: Boston City Council to weigh non-citizen voting
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Council to consider a petition allowing non-citizens with legal status to vote in municipal elections.
The petition, introduced by Councilor Julia Mejia, has support from seven other councilors and awaits a future committee hearing.
Non-citizens in Boston contribute $2.3 billion in taxes annually but are barred from voting, a point highlighted in the petition.

title: ‘He looks great’: Draymond Green had high praise for Jayson Tatum
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Draymond Green praised Jayson Tatum's performance after the Celtics' 120-99 victory over the Warriors.
Jayson Tatum scored 24 points with 10 rebounds, impressing Green with his post-injury form and athletic ability.
Green playfully engaged in trash talk with Tatum, showing camaraderie despite their competitive histories.

title: Two-time cancer survivor from Easton to run Boston Marathon for Mass General
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Kevin O'Rourke, 57, from Easton, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for Massachusetts General Hospital.
O'Rourke is running in honor of his late father, Brian, who received life-extending cancer treatment at Mass General.
A two-time cancer survivor, O'Rourke marks his first marathon since recovery, having previously run the Boston Marathon in 1997 and 1999.

title: Boston police officer arrested, charged with manslaughter in fatal shooting
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston police officer Nicholas O'Malley, 33, was arrested for manslaughter in the shooting death of Stephenson King, 39.
The fatal incident occurred on March 11 during a carjacking response in Roxbury; King allegedly hit a police cruiser.
Two city councilors have requested the release of body camera footage for transparency in the investigation.

title: Police fatally shoot person with knife at Weymouth park, DA says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Weymouth police shot and killed a person allegedly armed with a knife at Webb Memorial State Park.
The incident occurred after officers responded to a 911 call about a distressed individual on Wednesday afternoon.
The Massachusetts State Police is investigating the fatal shooting with no threat to public safety reported.
